methods.method_name: Sediment Processing for Benthic Foraminiferal Assemblages
methods.description: Process for examining sediment samples from mudline and core catcher for benthic foraminiferal assemblages, including washing, staining, and identifying organisms.

steps.description: Samples from mudline were sampled by unloading the matter from the top of each core into a bucket, washed with water through a 63-µm wire mesh sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: Unload and wash mudline samples through a 63-µm sieve

steps.description: One gram of Rose Bengal diluted in one liter of alcohol was applied to stain living organisms in the mudline samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Stain living organisms in mudline samples with Rose Bengal

steps.description: From each core, a catcher of 20-30 cm3 of sediment was washed with water over a 63-µm wire mesh sieve and were looked at for the presence of organisms.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash core catcher sediment and inspect for organisms

steps.description: Consolidated sediments were immersed in a 3% hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) solution, with little amount of Borax, prior to washing, to aid disaggregate hard material.
steps.searchable_summary: Disaggregate consolidated sediments with H2O2 and Borax

steps.description: Samples were dried in filter paper in low-temperature oven at ~50°C, and picked up with a fine brush under a binocular stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry samples and pick organisms under microscope

steps.description: To prevent mixing of organisms between samples, sieve was cleaned, set into a sonicator for 15 minutes, and inspected for organisms.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean sieve with sonicator and inspect

steps.description: Species picking and identification were made on the >150 µm size fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify species in >150 µm size fractions
